revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Gion shrines]][[ja:祇園神社]]
{{redirect|Gion Shrine|The Gion Shrine (Gion Jinja) formerly in Kyoto|Yasaka Shrine}} __NOTOC__ [[File:JP-Kyoto-yasaka.JPG|300px|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Higashiyama Ward, Kyoto)]] '''Gion Shrine''' (祇園神社, ''Gion Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} and {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノヲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, forming part of the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|qid=Q11588425|lt=Gion faith|1=Gion Faith}}. Shrines of the Gion faith are also known by other names, such as {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}} (including variations like 八阪神社 and 弥栄神社), {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|en|Hiromine Shrine|qid=Q11484399|lt=Hiromine Shrine|1=Hiromine Shrine}}, {{ill|Tenno Shrine|ja|天王神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenno Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}}, {{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}. The specific name used varies by era and source.
The name derives from the pre-[[Shinbutsu bunri]] period, when {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノヲ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, now enshrined as the principal deity, was identified with {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|qid=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery|1=Jetavana}}. Many shrines were also referred to as "Gozu Tennō Shrines" during this time.
With the enactment of Shinbutsu bunri in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, the Buddhist deity Gozu Tennō was removed as a worshipped figure, leaving the Shinto deity Susanoo. Concurrently, many shrines changed their names to omit Buddhist terms such as "Gion" and "Gozu Tennō." The main shrine in Kyoto, formerly known as Gion Shrine, was renamed {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}. Despite these changes, the terms "Gion," "Tennō," and references like {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|qid=Q979873|lt=Gion Matsuri|1=Gion Matsuri}} or "Gion Goryōe" remain prevalent in local festivals and as affectionate nicknames for these shrines.
Some Gion Shrines are called {{ill|Yakumo Shrines|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrines|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}}. The shrine's name derives from the {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}} verse recited by Susanoo: "Yakumo rises, the eightfold clouds enclose, in Izumo they build the eightfold fence to enclose my bride."<ref>https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E5%85%AB%E9%9B%B2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE</ref>
== Names by lineage ==
Shrines that were directly brought from {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395836}} often bear names such as {{ill|Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|須賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Suga Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11253400}}, {{ill|Yakumo Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}}, or {{ill|Izumo Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|出雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}}. In contrast, shrines originally dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}} under the {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|qid=Q11588425|lt=Gion faith|1=Gion Faith}} lineage were often renamed as {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Yasaka Shrine}}, {{ill|Gion Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|祇園神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Gion shrines|qid=Q11588442}}, or {{ill|Tsushima Shrine|en|Tsushima Shrine|qid=Q705136|lt=Tsushima Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Tsushima Shrine}} during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}. While all of these traditions involve Susanoo worship, {{ill|Hikawa Shrine|en|Hikawa Shrine|qid=Q5760456|lt=Hikawa shrines|1=Hikawa Shrine}} form a distinct and independent network.
== Gion Shrines across Japan == ; Kansai Region * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Aishō)|ja|祇園神社 (愛荘町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Aishō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504320|1=Gion Shrine (Aishō)}} ({{ill|Aishō, Shiga|en|Aishō, Shiga|qid=Q1346806|lt=Aishō|1=Aishō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Echi District, Shiga|ja|愛知郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echi District, Shiga|1=Aichi District|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11073856}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}) - Famous for its annual {{ill|fireworks festival|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fireworks festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=Fireworks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q127933}} on {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|qid=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}}. * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Hyōgo)|lt=Gion Shrine (Hyōgo)|lt=Gion Shrine (Kobe)|ja|祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Kobe)|qq=}} ({{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo Ward, Kobe|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}) - A shrine associated with [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, located at the base of Gion Mountain.
; Shikoku Region * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|ja|祇園神社 (吉野川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504323|1=Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)}} ({{ill|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|en|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|qid=Q592500|lt=Yoshinogawa|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}) - Located near the sacred {{ill|Kinokami Mountain|ja|忌部山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokami Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山川町 (吉野川市)|1=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28689703}}, which houses the {{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|qid=Q11490722|lt=Imibe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}}, a subordinate shrine.
; Kyushu Region * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Kurume)|ja|祇園神社 (久留米市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Kurume)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504325|1=Gion Shrine (Kurume)}} ({{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}) - Also known as Susanoo Shrine. * {{ill|Kitaoka Shrine|ja|北岡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaoka Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Nishi-ku|en|Nishi-ku|qid=Q296095|lt=Nishi-ku, Kumamoto|1=Nishi-ku}}, {{ill|Kumamoto|en|Kumamoto|qid=Q199889|lt=Kumamoto City|1=Kumamoto}}) - Designated as a {{ill|Chokugansha|ja|勅願社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chokugansha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勅願所|1=Chokugansho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22131160}}, receiving a special imperial title from {{ill|Emperor Konoe|en|Emperor Konoe|qid=Q373997|lt=Emperor Konoe|1=Emperor Konoe}} in 1154 (Kyuju 1). == Gion Shrines Across Japan == ; Kinki Region * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Aishō)|ja|祇園神社 (愛荘町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Aishō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504320|1=Gion Shrine (Aishō)}} ({{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Aishō, Shiga|en|Aishō, Shiga|qid=Q1346806|lt=Aishō, Shiga|1=Aishō, Shiga}}) - Famous for its annual {{ill|fireworks display|ja|花火大会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fireworks display|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花火|1=Fireworks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q127933}} on {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|qid=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}}. * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Hyōgo)|ja|祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Hyōgo)|qq=}} ({{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|en|Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1078790|lt=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}, Kamigion Town) - A shrine associated with [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, located at the foot of Mount Gion.
; Shikoku * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|ja|祇園神社 (吉野川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504323|1=Gion Shrine (Yoshinogawa)}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|en|Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|qid=Q592500|lt=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima|1=Yoshinogawa, Tokushima}}, Yamakawa Town, Anrakuji) - Situated near sacred mountains such as {{ill|Imbe Mountain|ja|忌部山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imbe Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山川町 (吉野川市)|1=Yamakawa Town (Yoshinogawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28689703}}. * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Ōzu)|ja|祇園神社 (大洲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Ōzu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504327|1=Gion Shrine (Ōzu)}} ({{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ōzu, Ehime|en|Ōzu, Ehime|qid=Q386655|lt=Ōzu, Ehime|1=Ōzu, Ehime}}) - Known for its winter Gion Festival held in late January.
; Chūgoku Region * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Kurashiki)|ja|祇園神社 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Kurashiki)|qq=}} ({{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki|1=Kurashiki}}, Shimotsui) - Located on the former site of {{ill|Shimotsui Castle|ja|下津井城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui Castle|1=Shimotsui Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11361097}} and revered during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} by {{ill|Kitamaebune|en|Kitamaebune|qid=Q3440099|lt=Kitamaebune|1=Kitamaebune}} traders. * {{ill|An Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja|安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An Shrine (Hiroshima)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504328|1=An Shrine (Hiroshima)}} (commonly called "Gion-san") ({{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|qid=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Asaminami-ku, Hiroshima|en|Asaminami-ku, Hiroshima|qid=Q63440|lt=Asaminami-ku, Hiroshima|1=Asaminami-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Gion|en|Gion|qid=Q926312|lt=Gion, Hiroshima|1=Gion}}) - Formerly "Gion-sha of Aki Province." The name "Gion" remains in local landmarks and institutions, such as {{ill|Gion Elementary School|ja|広島市立祇園小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Elementary School|1=Gion Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11484634}} and {{ill|Shimo-Gion Station|en|Shimo-Gion Station|qid=Q7496916|lt=Shimogion Station|1=Shimo-Gion Station}}.
; Kyushu Region * Gion Shrine ({{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|qid=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Kusano Town) - Known locally as {{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|須佐能袁神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Gion-sha ({{ill|Kanzaki, Saga|en|Kanzaki, Saga|qid=Q990392|lt=Kanzaki|1=Kanzaki, Saga}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|qid=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture|1=Saga Prefecture}}, Kanzaki Town) - A subsidiary shrine of {{ill|Kushida Shrine|ja|櫛田宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushida Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kitaoka Shrine|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaoka Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine (Nishiusuki)|ja|祇園神社 (五ヶ瀬町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine (Nishiusuki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504330|1=Gion Shrine (Nishiusuki)}} ({{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Gokase, Miyazaki|en|Gokase, Miyazaki|qid=Q1206765|lt=Gokase, Miyazaki|1=Gokase, Miyazaki}}, Kuraoka) - Known for its summer festival in mid-July and the autumn {{ill|Okunchi|ja|おくんち|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okunchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to くんち|1=kunchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11265757}} festival.
== Gion Faith Shrines with Different Names == * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} ({{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|qid=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi|1=Toyohashi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}) - A Gozu Tennō shrine historically referred to as "Ushiten Shrine." == Shrines with Different Names in Gion Faith == * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} ({{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Toyohashi|en|Toyohashi|qid=Q336431|lt=Toyohashi|1=Toyohashi}}, Sekiya Town) - Formerly known as Gozu Tennō Shrine. * {{ill|Tade Uga Shrine|ja|田出宇賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tade Uga Shrine|1=Tadeuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q85882438}} ({{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Minamiaizu|en|Minamiaizu|qid=Q1205058|lt=Minamiaizu|1=Minamiaizu}}) - Worships Susanoo and incorporates elements of Gion festivals.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)|ja|素盞嗚神社 (福山市新市町戸手)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine (Fukuyama)|qq=}} * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
}}
{{translated page|ja|祇園神社|version=100042244|comment=Imported full ja history}}
